serviceDescription: Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) is storage for the internet. You can use Amazon S3 to store and retrieve any amount of data at any time, from anywhere on the web.

templates.title: S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval
templates.description: is best used for data with unknown or changing access patterns.

cards.title: S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval
cards.helpText: S3 Glacier is a secure, durable, and low-cost storage class for data archiving. You can reliably store any amount of data at costs that are competitive with or cheaper than on-premises solutions.

components.label: S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval Average Object Size
components.description: Used to calculate number of objects which affects metadata overhead and cost of moving data into S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval. You can find the average object size of your existing S3 data using S3 Storage Lens in the S3 Console.

components.label: How will data be moved into S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval?

options.label: The specified amount of data is already stored in S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval

exists.meteredUnit: PUT requests to Glacier per Requests

options.label: PUT, COPY, POST, Lifecycle requests to S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval
options.id: PUT to Glacier Flexible Retrieval Requests

components.description: Automatically calculates PUT, COPY, POST, Lifecycle costs for moving data into S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval initially. To compare the cost of current storage in S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval to lifecycling this data to another storage class, you can specify that your storage is already stored in S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval while selecting Lifecycle under the new storage class to capture the upfront cost of moving your data.

components.label: Provisioned Capacity Units
components.description: Each unit of capacity provides that at least three expedited retrievals can be performed every five minutes and provides up to 150 MB/s of retrieval throughput.
